Block p005_c0008 merely lists BRT toolbox features introduced to workshop participants. No explicit statement attaches these features to any specific corridor or project. The block simply lists features and amenities.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Facilitators introduced the features and amenities that are part of the NYCDOT/NYCT's BRT toolbox. These included: Offset and Curbside Bus Lanes, Off-Board Fare Collection, Enhanced Stations, Real-time Arrival Information, BRT Vehicles, Subway-like Station Spacing, and Traffic Signal Priority for Buses.

rationale: Blocks p005_c0008 and p006_c0009 discuss enhanced stations as a BRT feature. p005_c0008 only lists it in the toolbox. p006_c0009 reports survey results showing participants preferred enhanced stations. Neither block explicitly states this treatment is attached to any specific corridor or project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Facilitators introduced the features and amenities that are part of the NYCDOT/NYCT's BRT toolbox. These included: Offset and Curbside Bus Lanes, Off-Board Fare Collection, Enhanced Stations, Real-time Arrival Information, BRT Vehicles, Subway-like Station Spacing, and Traffic Signal Priority for Buses.
In looking at the features and amenities that a BRT system could offer, the participants preferred Real Time Bus Information, Enhanced Stations, Off-set Bus Lanes and Off-Board Fair Collection.
